_source: AHA CPR Guidelines 2025, Emergency Nursing Procedures, Pharmacology texts
_note: Long-tail nursing data. Specific protocols, not generic care plans. Critical for ER/ICU.

vfib_vtach.immediate_action: Defibrillate 200J biphasic (360J monophasic); resume CPR immediately
vfib_vtach.second_amiodarone_dose: 150mg after 3-5 minutes if still in VF/VT

asystole_pea.immediate_action: High-quality CPR + search for reversible causes (H's and T's)
asystole_pea.epinephrine: 1mg IV/IO every 3-5 minutes as soon as access obtained

sepsis_protocol.septic_shock_definition: Sepsis + vasopressor needed to maintain MAP ≥65 + lactate >2 despite fluid resuscitation

hour_1_bundle.obtain_blood_cultures: Before antibiotics, but don't delay antibiotics >45 min
hour_1_bundle.apply_vasopressors: If hypotensive during or after fluid resuscitation; target MAP ≥65

antibiotic_selection.healthcare_associated_pneumonia: Pip-Tazo 4.5g q6h or Meropenem 1g q8h + Vancomycin

stroke_code.time_is_brain: 1.9 million neurons lost per minute in untreated stroke

cincinnati_stroke_scale.facial_droop: Ask to smile; one side doesn't move
cincinnati_stroke_scale.arm_drift: Close eyes, hold arms out; one arm drifts down
cincinnati_stroke_scale.speech: Repeat phrase 'You can't teach an old dog new tricks'; slurred or wrong words

tpa_eligibility.time_window: Within 4.5 hours of symptom onset (3 hours if >80 years old or anticoagulated)
tpa_eligibility.dose: 0.9 mg/kg (max 90mg); 10% IV bolus, rest over 60 minutes

stroke_code.thrombectomy_window: Up to 24 hours if large vessel occlusion + salvageable tissue on imaging

insulin.error_prevention: Never abbreviate 'U' as 'u' (looks like 0); always use 'units'

heparin.monitoring: aPTT 6 hours after rate change; goal 60-80 seconds
heparin.reversal: Protamine sulfate 1mg per 100 units heparin given in last 2-3 hours

potassium_iv.max_rate: 10 mEq/hour peripheral; 40 mEq/hour central with cardiac monitoring
potassium_iv.never_iv_push: Cardiac arrest risk; always dilute and infuse slowly

pain_management.reversal_agent: Naloxone (Narcan) 0.4mg IV; may repeat q2-3min; watch for re-sedation

news2.scoring: 0-20 scale; ≥7 = critical illness risk; trigger rapid response
news2.escalation_protocol: Score 5-6: increase monitoring; 7+: urgent MD review + consider ICU
